title: salix glauca subsp. glabrescens
intro: Salix glauca subsp. glabrescens, also known as the willow, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the family Salicaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to its high content of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Salix glauca subsp. glabrescens include relief from pain, fever reduction, and anti-inflammatory effects, making it a useful remedy for treating various conditions such as arthritis and menstrual cramps. Salix glauca subsp. glabrescens can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in folk medicine as a poultice or salve for topical application.
